long_description:
Multiple patches of updating ACL rules is not working for some endpoints.This is impacting CBR policies for IKS customers which might not take effect.
Incident appears to be intermittent, impacting ~10-20% of requests
short_description: Update of ACL rules is showing intermittent failures
close_notes: To migrate to the issue CSE team first created a new 3.5 database with similar configuration and generate new service credentials and dataplane certificates. Updated the credentials in Secret Manager, then brough down the CSE API server to prevent new updates to etcd. The team validate all changes thoroughly before rolling out updates to endpoints in other regions.
customer_facing_impact: - Users might not be able to add, edit or delete any CBR rules.
chronology:
Response Team Participants (add abbrev. role and name as needed)
=====================
CSE:  Mohan Kumar PB, Sahil Mahajan, Kiranbabu R A, Gurdial Singh
CSE MGT: Megha Bhalija
CSE EXEC: Ankur Srivastava, Shaival Choksi
IKS: Sohini Kar, Brad Behle, Ádám Mihelcsik, Santosh Kodandapani
STSM Ralph Bateman, Shaival Chokshi
ETCD: Pawan Sharma, Niall Flannigan
ACS ICD: Rumen Atanasov
ACS MGR: Georgi Toshkov
ICD: PETER COORIE
AVM: Ashok B, Shruthi A, Ian Jack and Nikolay Parov, James Gonzalez, Johanna Cook, Evelyn Artavia.

Slack(s): #containers-cie / https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/C4SN1JNG5/p1765258609914289
               #cloudserviceendpoint-cie / https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/C020QNHJLF7/p1765264794165129
               #service-endpoint-sre / https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/C060URT5761/p1764981501362529
               #warroom-cse-inc10509856 / https://ibm.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A2035KFEK
MS Teams: https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_NzRmYTBhMTEtNjk4Zi00YTE1LTg3OGYtNDI3OGJlOGI5MDc4%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%22fcf67057-50c9-4ad4-98f3-ffca64add9e9%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%224a1c4d11-adb9-460b-b810-afeee87364d3%22%7d


Times in UTC
[2025-DEC-09]
hh:mm  Impact Start
05:44 Sev3 CIE Confirmed
05:46 Joining as AVM OnDuty
05:50 Teams call opened by AVM with IKS and CSE SRE's. Discussion about service protection plan is ongoing.
06:10 Paging out ETCD team.
06:18 Container was restarted, currently in healthy status
06:22 ETCD joined the call
06:28 ETCD there was a converge images, which applied changes on the ETCD environment (normal operation), this would require pods to be restarted
06:29 SRE updated the issue is more on the reads, trying to identify which member has a bad data currently
06:32 ETCD will do a rolling restart of DBs
06:36 ETCD rolling restart is triggered and running now, once completed SRE will restart the proxy pods
06:38 ETCD updated, M0 is back online, M1 is currently restarting 
06:44 ETCD M0,M1, M2 are back online, now the leader is M1, was M2
06:45 ETCD will try moving the leader to M2, but it brings more alerts
07:11 CSE are seeing reads returning consistent data following a rolling restart of DB pods by etcd
07:32 IKS have identified resource to run their tests to see if the situation is mitigated
07:35 CSE are discussing what kind of alerts they can implement to avoid future recurrence
07:53 CSE are comparing Postgresql with etcd to see if any data is missing
07:57 IKS testing confirms that issue is not resolved
07:59 CSE confirm that the data is not complete
08:08 CSE will restart MISAPI proxy, as they are not able to reach out via cli to run commands
08:20 CSE are working to clarify the node members logic
08:28 CSE are again seeing errors in the etcd dashboard
08:39 CSE/ETCD discussing how to get consistent data across all members of the cluster.
08:48 CSE looking at a rebuild from postgres. It is possible that this will be disruptive and require downtime
08:50 IKS looking at the impact of a CSE downtime to fix this.
08:52 CSE are creating an ECR
09:00 CSE looking at the option to build a new etcd database from scratch, however the issue is that due to etcd being EOL, provisioning of a new cluster is blocked.
09:14 CSE are preparing the technical plan, prior to seeking exec approval
10:27 CSE team are continuing to test the plan in preprod
11:14 CSE have one DB running in staging. The need to provision additional resource. They will need to further test in production before announcing any required downtime 
12:30 etcd rebuild plan is defined (see recovery plan)
14:00 database instances created - deployment plan initialised in Staging
15:00 team having some issues with Ansible playbook in stagi
15:40 deployment in staging is completed.  
16:03 CSE MGT: Team is still completing few checks before api server is brought up on staging. We have not yet started with Production
16:50 preparing to start production (still doing some work in staging)
17:30 Vault updated with new etcd credentials
         Window for Prod;
         Environment: Production All regions
         Change Type: Emergency fix
         Impact: CSE team will be upgrading ETCD to version 3.5 in light of data inconsistency issue. This upgrade is required to address data inconsistency issues observed in the current database, which have been causing system instability and unpredictable behaviour. During this window, CSE team will bring down the API microservice , which in result will not allow users to create, delete, or update their existing endpoints. Existing Data path will have no impact.
Window: 9th Dec 4 PM UTC – 9th Dec 9 PM UTC
         CR:
         Dallas: CHG14245044
         Frankfurt: CHG14245045
         London: CHG14245047
         Sydney: CHG14245049
         Tokyo: CHG14245050
         Washington: CHG14245051
         Osaka: CHG14245052
         BNPP EU: CHG14245054
         Toronto: CHG14245055
18:00 AVM handover
18:14 team has completed the prep work. They are also working on the alarms that were generated as part of the prep work so the API server can be taken down.
18:29 CSE MGR - team has started with the control plane deployment. Starting with the CRs.
18:35 Changes have started running.
18:38 team will start CRs in au-syd region.
18:44 CSE team has moved to room 5 to validate some credentials.
18:46 API server is not down yet so customers would be able to do their operations, the team is debugging an error in the control plan deployment.
19:02 Shaival Choksi joining.
19:07 API server has gone down. us-south got some issues and the team was unable to bring down the PODs.
19:17 ACS team joining as there's a BNPP Sev2 case CS4425008. Warroom: bnpp_cs4425008_icd_vpe
19:19 CSE team has split and have moved some folks to BO4 to review BNPP case.
19:43 CSE continue working with ACS ICD team on the BNPP issue.
19:55 CSE confirmed team is going to start with step 6 from recovery plan "Run admin api restore to update new etcd  with the data; Ensure the data is restored fully"
21:06 The Database Team is still waiting for confirmation from the client regarding system assistance. Next step: We will wait for the client to confirm whether changes to the endpoint can be performed before proceeding.
21;13 The team is validating whether the proposed fix can be applied across all CSEs. They are also checking if adding a new role against the port will have any negative impact on the customer environment.
21:18 IKS team has confirmed that the cluster is in good condition. The call with BNPP has ended, and the case is now considered resolved.
21:23 The restore process is currently ongoing and is expected to complete within approximately 15 minutes.
21:38 Current Impact: Internal only. No customer-facing impact at this time.
Services dependent on this API for ACL updates are delayed, but external services remain unaffected.
21:40 Next Steps:
Await customer confirmation that the resolution is effective.
Remain available for any additional assistance if needed.
Impact:No ongoing impact. Services are operating normally.
22:08 the release has been completed
22:11 the service team will also run a validation to review the system
22:26 the team is now running chennai and them will be Sindney, San Paulo and the others.
22:45 Chenna and Sidney are done, Sao pablo are in progress, Toronto is also in progress,
22:55 Osaka is also done.
23:00 All regions are close to completing their recovery processes. Once this step is finalized, the team will perform a full validation and then bring the API server back online.
23:26 CSE team is progressing on the recovery plan as they continue running "update agent / update credentials for all endpoints with latest credentials" in all regions.
      Status:
      Che, Syd, Sao, Tor, Osaka, Fra, WDC have been completed.
      Lon, Dal, Mad, Tok are in progress.
23:37 San Jose, Par and BNPP are also in progress.
23:38 Tok, Dal completed.
23:42 CSE confirming API server was back 30min back (~23:10UTC).
23:50 CSE - All regions have the updated seagent binary which will pull data from new etcd instance
23:55 CSE has confirmed service is back on line.
===2025-12-10===
00:05 STSM Shaival Chokshi confirmed CIE can be closed.

=============================
               FOR AVM REFERENCE
=============================
